parameters.name: .proplist

parameters.name: .proplist

parameters.name: .proplist

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of properties to return
parameters.name: .proplist

connection-count.description: Number of TCP connections to use

local-tx-speed.description: Transfer test maximum speed (bits per second)

local-udp-tx-size.description: Local transmit packet size in bytes

password.description: Password for the remote user (string value, max length 32)

random-data.description: If random-data is set to yes, the payload of the bandwidth test packets will have incompressible random data stream so that links that use data compression will not distort the results (this is CPU intensive and random-data should be set to no for low speed CPUs)

remote-tx-speed.description: Receive test maximum speed (bits per second)

remote-udp-tx-size.description: Remote transmit packet size in bytes

address.description: The address that will be used for "A" or "AAAA" type records. (A.B.C.D    (IP address))

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of properties to return
parameters.name: .proplist

file.description: List of the file names that will be attached to the mail separated by a comma.
List of the file names that will be attached to the mail separated by a comma.

certificate-verification.description: Enables trust chain validation from local certificate store. yes-without-crl, validates a certificate, not performing CRL check (certificate revocation list).

from.description: Name or email address that will be shown as a receiver. (string value)

password.description: Password used for authenticating to an SMTP server. (string value, max length 255)

server.description: Ip or IPv6 address of SMTP server. If not specified, a value from the server's configuration is used. (see documentation)

tls.description: Whether to use TLS encryption:yes - sends STARTTLS and drops the session if TLS is not available on the serverno - do not send STARTTLSstarttls - sends STARTTLS and continue without TLS if a server responds that TLS is not available

user.description: The username used for authenticating to an SMTP server. (string value, max length 255)

vrf.description: Set VRF on which service is creating outgoing connections.

accept-icmp-time-exceeded.description: If the ICMP "time exceeded" message should be considered a valid response

certificate.description: Certificate from local store that should be used for host verification.

check-certificate.description: Enables trust chain validation from local certificate store.

dns-server.description: The DNS server that the probe should send its requests to, if not specified it will use the value from "/ip dns".

down-script.description: Script to execute on the event of probe state change 'OK' --> 'fail' (string value)

early-failure-detection.description: Netwatch will not wait to finish all the packets to be processed to change probe status if it is already known that host will be considered as "down".

early-success-detection.description: Netwatch will not wait to finish all the packets to be processed to change probe status if it is already known that host will be considered as "up".

host.description: The IP address of the server to be probed. Formats:- ipv4- ipv4@vrf- ipv6- ipv6@vrf- ipv6-linklocal%interface- domain name (for type=dns)

ignore-initial-down.description: Specifies if "Down" script should be ran if probe state change goes from `Unknown` to 'Down'. "no" means that change from 'Unknown' to 'Down' will not be ignored.Should be used with care, as first "Down" status won't be executed, and Down script will only be ran if probe goes through "Up" > "Down" state.

ignore-initial-up.description: Specifies if "Up" script should be ran if probe state change goes from `Unknown` to 'Up', used to help against false positives after enabling the probe, or after a reboot. "no" means that change from Unknown to Up will not be ignored.

interval.description: The time interval between probe tests (1s..71w2h27m52s950ms    (time interval))

name.description: user added name for the Netwatch entry (string value)

packet-count.description: Total count of ICMP packets to send out within a single test

packet-interval.description: The time between ICMP-request packet send (20ms..71w2h27m52s950ms    (time interval))

packet-size.description: The total size of the IP ICMP packet

port.description: TCP port (for both tcp-conn and http-get probes)

record-type.description: Record type that will be used for DNS probe.

src-address.description: Source IP address which the Netwatch will try to use in order to reach the host. If address is not present, then the host will be considered as "down".

start-delay.description: Time to wait before starting probe (on add, enable, or system start) (0s..1h    (time interval))

startup-delay.description: Time to wait until starting Netwatch probe after system startup (0s..1h    (time interval))

test-script.description: Script to execute at the end of every probe test (string value)

thr-avg.description: Fail threshold for rtt-avg (round trip time-avg) (time interval)

thr-max.description: Fail threshold for rtt-max (a value above thr-max is a probe fail) (time interval)

thr-tcp-conn-time.description: Fail threshold for tcp-connect-time, the configuration uses microseconds, if the time unit is not specified (s/m/h), log and status pages display the same value in milliseconds. (0s..30s    (time interval))

timeout.description: Max time limit to wait for a response (10ms..4w    (time interval))

ttl.description: Manually set time to live value for ICMP packet

type.description: Type of the probe:- icmp - (ping-style) series of ICMP request-response with statistics- tcp-conn - test TCP connection (3-way handshake) to a server specified by IP and port- http-get - do an HTTP Get request and test for a range of correct replies- https-get - do an HTTP Get request and test for a range of correct replies- dns - do a specified DNS query for domain name- simple - simplified ICMP probe, with fewer options than "ICMP" type, used for backward compatibility with the older Netwatch version

up-script.description: Script to execute on the event of probe state change 'fail' --> 'OK' (string value)

interval.description: The time interval between probe tests (00:00:00.200..    (time interval))

accept-icmp-time-exceeded.description: If the ICMP "time exceeded" message should be considered a valid response

certificate.description: Certificate from local store that should be used for host verification.

check-certificate.description: Enables trust chain validation from local certificate store.

dns-server.description: The DNS server that the probe should send its requests to, if not specified it will use the value from "/ip dns".

down-script.description: Script to execute on the event of probe state change 'OK' --> 'fail' (string value)

early-failure-detection.description: Netwatch will not wait to finish all the packets to be processed to change probe status if it is already known that host will be considered as "down".

early-success-detection.description: Netwatch will not wait to finish all the packets to be processed to change probe status if it is already known that host will be considered as "up".

host.description: The IP address of the server to be probed. Formats:- ipv4- ipv4@vrf- ipv6- ipv6@vrf- ipv6-linklocal%interface- domain name (for type=dns)

ignore-initial-down.description: Specifies if "Down" script should be ran if probe state change goes from `Unknown` to 'Down'. "no" means that change from 'Unknown' to 'Down' will not be ignored.Should be used with care, as first "Down" status won't be executed, and Down script will only be ran if probe goes through "Up" > "Down" state.

ignore-initial-up.description: Specifies if "Up" script should be ran if probe state change goes from `Unknown` to 'Up', used to help against false positives after enabling the probe, or after a reboot. "no" means that change from Unknown to Up will not be ignored.

packet-size.description: The total size of the IP ICMP packet

startup-delay.description: Time to wait until starting Netwatch probe after system startup (0s..1h    (time interval))

timeout.description: Max time limit to wait for a response (10ms..4w    (time interval))

ttl.description: Manually set time to live value for ICMP packet

parameters.name: .proplist

file-limit.description: File size limit. Sniffer will stop when a limit is reached.

file-name.description: Name of the file where sniffed packets will be saved.

filter-cpu.description: CPU core used as a filter.

filter-direction.description: Specifies which direction filtering will be applied.

filter-dst-ip-address.description: Up to 16 IP destination addresses used as a filter.

filter-dst-ipv6-address.description: Up to 16 IPv6 destination addresses used as a filter.

filter-dst-mac-address.description: Up to 16 MAC destination addresses and MAC address masks used as a filter.

filter-dst-port.description: Up to 16 comma-separated destination ports used as a filter. A list of predefined port names is also available, like ssh and telnet.

filter-interface.description: Interface name on which sniffer will be running. all indicates that the sniffer will sniff packets on all interfaces.

filter-ip-address.description: Up to 16 IP addresses used as a filter.

filter-ip-protocol.description: Up to 16 comma-separated IP/IPv6 protocols used as a filter. IP protocols (instead of protocol names, protocol numbers can be used):ipsec-ah - IPsec AH protocolipsec-esp - IPsec ESP protocolddp - datagram delivery protocolegp - exterior gateway protocolggp - gateway-gateway protocolgre - general routing encapsulationhmp - host monitoring protocolidpr-cmtp - idpr control message transporticmp - internet control message protocolicmpv6 - internet control message protocol v6igmp - internet group management protocolipencap - ip encapsulated in ipipip - ip encapsulationencap - ip encapsulationiso-tp4 - iso transport protocol class 4ospf - open shortest path firstpup - parc universal packet protocolpim - protocol independent multicastrspf - radio shortest path firstrdp - reliable datagram protocolst - st datagram modetcp - transmission control protocoludp - user datagram protocolvmtp - versatile message transportvrrp - virtual router redundancy protocolxns-idp - xerox xns idpxtp - xpress transfer protocol

filter-ipv6-address.description: Up to 16 IPv6 addresses used as a filter.

filter-mac-address.description: Up to 16 MAC addresses and MAC address masks used as a filter.

filter-mac-protocol.description: Up to 16 comma separated entries used as a filter. Mac protocols (instead of protocol names, protocol number can be used):802.2 - 802.2 Frames (0x0004)arp - Address Resolution Protocol (0x0806)capsman - CAPsMAN to CAP MAC layer connection (0x88BB)dot1x - EAPoL IEEE 802.1X (0x888E)homeplug-av - HomePlug AV MME (0x88E1)ip - Internet Protocol version 4 (0x0800)ipv6 - Internet Protocol Version 6 (0x86DD)ipx - Internetwork Packet Exchange (0x8137)lacp - Link Aggregation Control Protocol (0x8809)lldp - Link Layer Discovery Protocol (0x88CC)loop-protect - Loop Protect Protocol (0x9003)macsec - MAC security IEEE 802.1AE (0x88E5)mpls-multicast - MPLS multicast (0x8848)mpls-unicast - MPLS unicast (0x8847)mvrp - Multiple VLAN Registration protocol (0x88F5)packing-compr - Encapsulated packets with compressed IP packing (0x9001)packing-simple - Encapsulated packets with simple IP packing (0x9000)pppoe - PPPoE Session Stage (0x8864)pppoe-discovery - PPPoE Discovery Stage (0x8863)rarp - Reverse Address Resolution Protocol (0x8035)romon - Router Management Overlay Network RoMON (0x88BF)service-vlan - Provider Bridging (IEEE 802.1ad) & Shortest Path Bridging IEEE 802.1aq (0x88A8)vlan - VLAN-tagged frame (IEEE 802.1Q) and Shortest Path Bridging IEEE 802.1aq with NNI compatibility (0x8100)

filter-operator-between-entries.description: Changes the logic for filters with multiple entries.

filter-port.description: Up to 16 comma-separated ports used as a filter. A list of predefined port names is also available, like ssh and telnet.

filter-size.description: Filters packets of specified size or size range in bytes.

filter-src-ip-address.description: Up to 16 IP source addresses used as a filter.

filter-src-ipv6-address.description: Up to 16 IPv6 source addresses used as a filter.

filter-src-mac-address.description: Up to 16 MAC source addresses and MAC address masks used as a filter.

filter-src-port.description: Up to 16 comma-separated source ports used as a filter. A list of predefined port names is also available, like ssh and telnet.

filter-stream.description: Sniffed packets that are devised for the sniffer server are ignored.

filter-vlan.description: Up to 16 VLAN IDs used as a filter.

memory-limit.description: Memory amount used to store sniffed data.

memory-scroll.description: Whether to rewrite older sniffed data when the memory limit is reached.

only-headers.description: Save in the memory only the packet's headers, not the whole packet.

streaming-enabled.description: Defines whether to send sniffed packets to the streaming server.

streaming-server.description: Tazmen Sniffer Protocol (TZSP) stream receiver. (A.B.C.D    (IP address))

parameters.name: .proplist

streaming-server.description: Tazmen Sniffer Protocol (TZSP) stream receiver. (A.B.C.D    (IP address))

address.description: Addresses from which connections to SNMP server is allowed (A.B.C.D    (IP address))

authentication-password.description: Password used to authenticate the connection to the server (SNMPv3) (string value)

encryption-password.description: the password used for encryption (SNMPv3) (string value)

encryption-protocol.description: encryption protocol to be used to encrypt the communication (SNMPv3). AES (see rfc3826) available since v6.16.

address.description: Addresses from which connections to SNMP server is allowed (A.B.C.D    (IP address))

authentication-password.description: Password used to authenticate the connection to the server (SNMPv3) (string value)

encryption-password.description: the password used for encryption (SNMPv3) (string value)

encryption-protocol.description: encryption protocol to be used to encrypt the communication (SNMPv3). AES (see rfc3826) available since v6.16.

parameters.name: .proplist

disabled.description: Whether the port is disabled and does not participate in receiving/sending the packets

interface.description: Optional parameter of packet template. This is mutually exclusive with the "port" setting. Specifying "interface" allows users not to create a port entry for interface in the port menu. In fact, a port entry is created dynamically. This is useful for running quick tests.

name.description: Descriptive name of the template. (string value)

disabled.description: Whether the port is disabled and does not participate in receiving/sending the packets

interface.description: Optional parameter of packet template. This is mutually exclusive with the "port" setting. Specifying "interface" allows users not to create a port entry for interface in the port menu. In fact, a port entry is created dynamically. This is useful for running quick tests.

name.description: Descriptive name of the template. (string value)

disabled.description: Whether the port is disabled and does not participate in receiving/sending the packets

interface.description: Optional parameter of packet template. This is mutually exclusive with the "port" setting. Specifying "interface" allows users not to create a port entry for interface in the port menu. In fact, a port entry is created dynamically. This is useful for running quick tests.

name.description: Descriptive name of the template. (string value)

parameters.name: .proplist

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of properties to return
parameters.name: .proplist

latency-distribution-max.description: Maximal latency range for latency distribution measurements. Based on this value, RouterOS will decide what latency range to use as the latency-distribution-measure-interval property (time interval)

measure-out-of-order.description: Whether to measure Out-of-Order packets. The default value is based on CPU type (multi-core CPU default no; single-core CPU default yes). When the property is enabled on the multi-core device, a single stream will utilize only a single CPU core

stats-samples-to-keep.description: How many data examples to collect

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of properties to return
parameters.name: .proplist

port.description: Optional parameter of packet template. This suggests a port through which packets generated using this template should be sent out. Port can also be specified in other places such as in stream settings. This is mutually exclusive with interface setting.

mbps.description: Value in Megabits per second that stream will try to generate.

name.description: Descriptive name of the template. (string value)

packet-size.description: Generated the size of the packets in bytes. Can be set as the range for random packet size generation.

port.description: Optional parameter of packet template. This suggests a port through which packets generated using this template should be sent out. Port can also be specified in other places such as in stream settings. This is mutually exclusive with interface setting.

pps.description: Packets per second that stream will try to generate.

tx-template.description: Name of the packet template from packet-template or raw-packet-template menus used as the packet content source.
